methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in AI & Software (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 16 same-sector peers exist in AI & Software, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
HUMAIN is a sovereign-stage AI & Software company backed by PIF, having raised $40B+ in capitalized commitments. The valuation universe comprises 16 peers within AI & Software, including 1 public comparable (Palantir, PLTR) and 8 private peers such as Waymo, Figure AI, Scale AI, and Reflection AI.

Peer-median triangulation is not applicable here. The narrow filter—requiring same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier peers—yielded fewer than three datapoints necessary for statistical validity. While 16 same-sector peers exist, none meet the stage and tier criteria simultaneously. A fallback to all-sector median would be uninformative given the heterogeneity of business models, maturity, and capital structures within AI & Software.

This analysis does not support a specific valuation range. The primary constraint is the absence of sufficiently comparable precedents at HUMAIN's sovereign scale and stage. Future valuation work would require either: (1) expansion of the comp set to include later-stage private or public peers with disclosed metrics, or (2) alternative methods such as discounted cash flow or precedent transaction analysis with explicit assumptions. The key uncertainty remains the lack of publicly available financial or operational data for HUMAIN itself, which would be essential to apply any standardized multiple or cash-flow-based approach.
